message:
🌱 Add k8up: Support "auto" schedules mission (#1069)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,77 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "k8up-116-support-auto-schedules",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "k8up: Support \"auto\" schedules",
+    "description": "Support \"auto\" schedules. Community-requested feature.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current k8up deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your k8up version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n k8up -l app.kubernetes.io/name=k8up\nhelm list -n k8up 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working k8up installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review k8up configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant k8up configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n k8up -l app.kubernetes.io/name=k8up\nkubectl get configmap -n k8up -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=k8up\n```\n**As** K8up user\\\n**I want** to specify smart schedules\\\n**So that** I can let K8up figure out optimal schedules for optimal resource usage"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Support \"auto\" schedules",
+        "description": "Implements new schedules:\n* ``\n* ``\n* ``\n* ``\n* `` or ``\n\nSpecial case: Monthly. The `` will produce a schedule with a day-of-month between 1 and 27, even though some months have 30 and 31 days. This is to prevent schedules being skipped if the current month has fewer days than specified in the\n```yaml\nmetadata:\n  ...\nstatus:\n  effectiveSchedules:\n    archive: 0 * * * *\n    check: 44 * * * *\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n k8up -l app.kubernetes.io/name=k8up\nkubectl get events -n k8up --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Support \"auto\" schedules\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Implements new schedules:\n* ``\n* ``\n* ``\n* ``\n* `` or ``\n\nSpecial case: Monthly. The `` will produce a schedule with a day-of-month between 1 and 27, even though some months have 30 and 31 days. This is to prevent schedules being skipped if the current month has fewer days than specified in the day-of-month field in the schedule.\n\nImplemented status field for random schedules, e.g.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "metadata:\n  ...\nstatus:\n  effectiveSchedules:\n    archive: 0 * * * *\n    check: 44 * * * *"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
